title: Smoky Roasted Polish Pork Skillet
description: A hearty and flavorful Polish dish featuring roasted pork, cilantro leaves, and a hint of smokiness, all cooked to perfection in a traditional ttukbaegi earthenware pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork shoulder with smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 20 minutes per pound, or until tender and flaky.
Stir in the chopped cilantro leaves and serve hot.
Let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
